---
name: nezam-api-design
description: OpenAPI 3.1 contracts, REST/GraphQL guidelines, versioning, idempotency, and error schemas before implementation.
version: 1.0.0
updated: 2026-05-08
changelog: []
---

# Purpose

Author API contracts that are typed, versioned, idempotent, and consumer-friendly. Owns the OpenAPI 3.1 (or GraphQL SDL) source of truth before any handler is implemented. Single-responsibility: API contract spec.

# Inputs

- Domain entities + use cases from `docs/core/required/prd/PRD.md` and `docs/core/required/features/`.
- Data model from `@.cursor/skills/nezam-prisma-orm/SKILL.md` or `@.cursor/skills/nezam-supabase-architect/SKILL.md`.
- Auth model from `@.cursor/skills/nezam-auth-workflows/SKILL.md`.
- Performance & cache constraints from `@.cursor/skills/nezam-cache-strategies/SKILL.md`.

# Step-by-Step Workflow

1. Choose API style per use case: REST (resource-oriented, broad reach), GraphQL (complex aggregation, typed clients), gRPC (internal high-throughput).
2. Author OpenAPI 3.1 spec (`docs/core/required/api/openapi.yaml`) or GraphQL SDL (`schema.graphql`); include components, parameters, responses, security schemes.
3. Define versioning: URI (`/v1/`) or media-type; document deprecation policy + sunset headers.
4. Standardize error schema: RFC 9457 `application/problem+json` (`type`, `title`, `status`, `detail`, `instance`, extensions).
5. Specify idempotency: `Idempotency-Key` header for POST mutations; document storage and TTL.
6. Add pagination (cursor + page-size), filtering (`filter[field]=`), sorting (`sort=field,-other`), sparse fields (`fields[type]=`).
7. Validate spec: lint with Spectral, generate clients & types; commit generated types to `packages/api/`.

# Validation & Metrics

- Spec passes Spectral with zero blocking findings.
- Every endpoint has request schema, response schema (per status), and security definition.
- Errors return `application/problem+json` with stable `type` URIs.
- Idempotency keys honored for ≥ 24h.
- Backward-compat tests detect breaking changes between versions.

# Output Format

- `docs/core/required/api/openapi.yaml` (OpenAPI 3.1) and/or `schema.graphql`.
- Generated client/types under `packages/api/`.
- API style guide + error catalogue.
- Versioning + deprecation policy.

# Integration Hooks

- `/PLAN api` produces the contract.
- `/DEVELOP` builds handlers against the spec (without writing app code here).
- `/SCAN security` validates auth and input handling.
- Pairs with `@.cursor/skills/nezam-api-gateway/SKILL.md`, `@.cursor/skills/nezam-auth-workflows/SKILL.md`, `@.cursor/skills/nezam-cache-strategies/SKILL.md`.
- Honors `[.cursor/rules/workspace-orchestration.mdc](.cursor/rules/workspace-orchestration.mdc)`.

# Anti-Patterns

- Hand-written types out of sync with the spec.
- Mixing snake_case and camelCase in the same surface.
- Unversioned breaking changes pushed to production.
- Errors returned as plain strings or HTML.
- POST mutations without idempotency on retried networks.

# External Reference

- OpenAPI Specification 3.1 (https://spec.openapis.org/oas/v3.1.0) — current.
- RFC 9457 Problem Details for HTTP APIs (https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9457) — current.
- GraphQL spec (https://spec.graphql.org/) — current.
- Spectral linter (https://stoplight.io/open-source/spectral) — current.
- Closest skills.sh/official analog: api-design / openapi.
